findings.title: Scanner hardcodes 'data/' instead of using dataDir(), ignoring DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT
findings.description: At L260, the fallback that loads per-project ignorePaths uses path.resolve(`data/${params.projectId}/config.json`) with a hardcoded 'data/' prefix. The rest of the codebase (paths.ts, processor, sandbox orchestrator, init-project, export, triage) consistently uses dataDir(projectId) which honors process.env.DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT. When DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT is set to anything other than 'data', this fallback silently misses the user's config.json (the catch swallows ENOENT/JSON errors), so config.json:ignorePaths is never applied. Because the IGNORE_DIRS defaults are then the only filter, files the user explicitly told the scanner to skip will be scanned and stored as candidate snippets in data/<id>/files/*.json. If those excluded paths contain credentials, secrets, or other sensitive content, the snippets land in the local mirror — a data-handling concern even though the directory is gitignored. Recommend: import dataDir from @deepsec/core and use path.resolve(dataDir(params.projectId), 'config.json'), matching the rest of the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Replace `path.resolve(\`data/${params.projectId}/config.json\`)` with `path.resolve(dataDir(params.projectId), 'config.json')`, importing dataDir from @deepsec/core. This unifies the data-root resolution with the rest of the codebase and ensures DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT is honored.

revalidation.reasoning: The current scanner imports dataDir from @deepsec/core and uses path.resolve(dataDir(params.projectId), "config.json") when loading project config ignorePaths. In packages/core/src/paths.ts, dataDir(projectId) is backed by getDataRoot(), which returns process.env.DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT or falls back to "data", so the environment override is honored. I searched the scanner file for the reported hardcoded data/${params.projectId}/config.json pattern and did not find it. The remaining comments mention data/<projectId>/config.json as documentation, not as a path construction bug. With the current code, a user setting DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT will have the same config root used by this fallback and the rest of the core path helpers. I could not cite the patch commit because the sandbox lacks git metadata, but the vulnerable line described in the finding has been replaced in the current source.
